title: CursorBench 3.1
author: handfuloflight
relevanceReason: 帖子介绍了 CursorBench 3.1 基准测试，评估多个 AI 模型在真实 Cursor 会话中的多文件任务表现，涉及 AI 编程代理、模型性能与成本对比，属于 AI 与开发工具交叉领域。
summaryRaw: We evaluate agents on ambiguous, multi-file tasks from real Cursor sessions. Higher scores are better.More about CursorBenchA scatter and line chart comparing Fable 5, Opus 4.8, Opus 4.7, GPT-5.5, Sonnet 5, Sonnet 4.6, GLM 5.2, Composer 2.5, and Composer 2 scores against Low41.5%$1.8921,2115036Kimi 2.531.9%$0.879,44630ChangelogCursorBench 3.1Introduced problems focused on codebase understanding, bugfinding, planning, and code review.Improved grading criteria for some edit tasks.CursorBench 3.0Initial set of tasks focused on edit, refactor, and bugfix problems.Avg cost / task is computed by applying each model's published per-million-token pricing (input, cache read, cache write, and output) to the tokens it used on each CursorBench 3.1 task, then averaging across tasks. Results are subject to variance; small differences in scores may not be statistically meaningful.
summaryZh: Cursor 发布 CursorBench 3.1，用于评估 AI 编程代理在模糊、多文件真实开发任务中的表现。
测试涵盖代码库理解、Bug 查找、规划和代码审查等能力，并优化了编辑任务的评分标准。
对比了 Fable 5、Opus 4.8、GPT-5.5、Sonnet 5、GLM 5.2 等多个模型的性能与平均任务成本。
highlightsZh: Fable 5 Max 以 72.9% 得分领先，但单任务成本高达 18.02 美元。
Composer 2.5 以 63.2% 得分和 0.55 美元成本展现极高性价比。
新版本引入代码库理解、Bug 定位、规划和代码审查等更贴近真实开发的测试场景。
模型得分与成本呈正相关，但部分模型在中等配置下表现优异，适合不同预算场景。
基准测试基于真实 Cursor 会话数据，更贴近开发者实际使用体验。

comments.author: mdasen
comments.textRawHtml:
I'm a bit skeptical.

Cursor's benchmark finds that Cursor's model (Composer 2.5) is basically as good as Opus 4.8 max and GPT-5.5 xhigh, but at a fraction of the price.

Artificial Analysis' testing shows Composer 2.5 to be pretty far behind: https://artificialanalysis.ai/agents/coding-agents. You look at the DeepSWE benchmark (which is probably the hardest to game at this point) and GPT-5.5 xhigh gets a 64, Opus 4.8 max gets 56, and Cursor 2.5 gets 16.

I don't doubt that Cursor works well for some people. It's beating DeepSeek v4 Pro in the DeepSWE benchmark and that's a very capable model. But I'm skeptical of the claims that it's a competitor for Opus 4.8 and GPT-5.5. It just seems convenient that their model does so well on their own benchmark while third party benchmarks have it far behind. Maybe it's a really great benchmark and a better measure than third party ones - I'd love for a cheap model to do as well as the expensive ones.
comments.textZhHtml:
我有点持怀疑态度。

Cursor的基准测试发现，Cursor的模型（Composer 2.5）基本上和Opus 4.8 max以及GPT-5.5 xhigh一样好，但价格却只有它们的一小部分。

Artificial Analysis的测试显示，Composer 2.5的表现要差得多：https://artificialanalysis.ai/agents/coding-agents。你看看DeepSWE基准测试（这可能是目前最难作弊的），GPT-5.5 xhigh得了64分，Opus 4.8 max得了56分，而Cursor 2.5只得了16分。

我不怀疑Cursor对某些人来说很好用。它在DeepSWE基准测试中击败了DeepSeek v4 Pro，而那是一个能力很强的模型。但我对它声称能与Opus 4.8和GPT-5.5竞争持怀疑态度。他们自己的模型在自己的基准测试上表现这么好，而在第三方基准测试中却差得远，这未免太巧了。也许这真的是一个非常棒的基准测试，比第三方的更能说明问题——我倒真希望有个便宜的模型能表现得和贵的一样好。

children.author: leerob
children.textRawHtml:
(I work at Cursor) When Composer 2.5 launched, we initially scored very competitively on AA's composite benchmark. I believe 3rd place overall. They have recently updated to use DeepSWE, which has more of a focus on very long-horizon tasks, and Composer isn't as good at those yet. We're aware and working on this for our next model.

Overall, some benchmarks show Composer doing well, others not so much. We think the model is very capable at the given price point. There's lots to improve! If you see any specific behaviors or places the model isn't very good, lmk here or can email me lrobinson at cursor.com.
children.textZhHtml:
（我在Cursor工作）Composer 2.5刚发布时，我们在AA的综合基准测试中得分非常有竞争力。我记得是总体第三名。他们最近更新了测试，改用DeepSWE，该测试更侧重于超长周期任务，而Composer在这方面目前还不够好。我们意识到了这个问题，并正在为下一代模型改进。

总的来说，有些基准测试显示Composer表现不错，有些则不然。我们认为该模型在同等价位下非常强大。还有很多需要改进的地方！如果你发现任何具体的行为问题或模型表现不佳的地方，请在这里留言，或者发邮件到lrobinson@cursor.com联系我。

children.author: giancarlostoro
children.textRawHtml:
How does it compare to a $100 Claude subscription at $60? Especially in terms of how much of it I can use, because I havent found anything that is in the US that can get me similar usage as Claude at $100 per month or less, really open to alternatives.

Grok build only gave me roughly 10 hours of use for $40 for the entire month...

I don't even care about long horizon, can I use it a reasonable amount of time through the month? I use AI for hobby projects, Claude gets me quite far, but I tire of dropping $100 every month. I'm not sending my money to some Chinese firm that now has access to my computer.
children.textZhHtml:
与 100 美元的 Claude 订阅（60 美元）相比如何？特别是在我可以使用多少方面，因为我在美国还没有找到任何东西可以让我以每月 100 美元或更少的价格获得与 Claude 类似的使用，真正愿意接受替代方案。

Grok build 整个月只给了我大约 10 个小时的使用时间，价格为 40 美元......

我什至不关心长期视野，我可以在整个月使用它合理的时间吗？我将人工智能用于业余爱好项目，Claude 让我走得很远，但我厌倦了每月花 100 美元。我不会把钱汇给现在可以访问我的计算机的中国公司。

children.author: artooro
children.textRawHtml: I never run long horizon tasks. So Composer 2.5 is great.
children.textZhHtml: 我从不运行长期任务。所以 Composer 2.5 很棒。

children.author: forgot-my-pw
children.textRawHtml: Even with the new benchmark, Composer 2.5 seems to be just a bit worse than Opus 4.7. So I assume it's going to be about similar with Sonnet 5.0 at 1/6 of the cost.
children.textZhHtml: 即使采用新的基准测试，Composer 2.5 似乎也只比 Opus 4.7 差一点。所以我认为它与 Sonnet 5.0 差不多，但成本只有 Sonnet 5.0 的 1/6。

children.author: ai_slop_hater
children.textRawHtml: Don't lie. You forked a Chinese model.
children.textZhHtml: 不要撒谎。你分叉了一个中国模型。

children.author: CuriouslyC
children.textRawHtml:
Not hard to understand what's going on here. They RL'd around patterns in their data and specific capabilities, so of course they'd construct a benchmark that's aligned with the training set.

Ironically, their benchmark might be more accurate than artificial analysis for a narrow slice of things that Cursor's Eigencustomer is really interested in. Otherwise I'd take it as just another data point.
children.textZhHtml:
这里面的门道不难理解。他们针对数据中的模式和特定能力进行了强化学习，所以自然会构建一个与训练集分布一致的基准测试。

讽刺的是，对于Cursor的核心用户真正关心的那一小部分特定场景，他们的基准测试可能比Artificial Analysis的更准确。否则的话，我就只把它当作又一个参考数据点了。

children.author: leerob
children.textRawHtml: (I work at Cursor) CursorBench includes many evals from actual engineering tasks from the Cursor team, which include our private codebase. This codebase is held-out from training so models haven't seen it, including Composer.
children.textZhHtml: （我在 Cursor 工作）CursorBench 包含来自 Cursor 团队实际工程任务的许多评估，其中包括我们的私人代码库。该代码库未经过训练，因此模型（包括 Composer）看不到它。

children.author: burmanm
children.textRawHtml:
DeepSWE is slightly flawed in the sense that is uses only its own harness and that causes issues on models that are not correctly supported by it. There's huge amount of evidence that the harness plays a big role in how these models work and yet DeepSWE entirely removes that (and has probably only tested that it works fine with some favourite model of them).

There's also issues with cost calculation (as that harness doesn't use caches) and so on as reported on their github issues.

None of the benchmarks are perfect, but that does explain a lot of the variations between benchmarks.
children.textZhHtml:
DeepSWE稍微有点缺陷，因为它只使用自己的测试框架，这会导致在不被该框架正确支持的模型上出现问题。有大量证据表明测试框架对这些模型的表现有很大影响，而DeepSWE完全忽略了这一点（而且可能只测试了它和他们最喜欢的模型配合良好）。

成本计算也有问题（因为该框架不使用缓存），他们的GitHub issue里也有相关报告。

没有哪个基准测试是完美的，但这确实解释了很多基准测试之间的差异。

children.author: extr
children.textRawHtml: I think DeepSWE is flawed in a different way: the tasks look like someone took a bunch of big highly technical PRs they found really well done, and inverted it into specs for agents to autistically execute. This is not really how people use agents in practice IMO. And it's why DeepSWE is so generous to OAI models, rigid task execution is the thing they're best at. I think FrontierCode matches the vibes a lot better.
children.textZhHtml: 我认为 DeepSWE 在另一个方面存在缺陷：这些任务看起来像是有人拿了一堆他们认为做得很好的大型高技术 PR，并将其反转为代理自动执行的规范。在我看来，这并不是人们在实践中使用代理的方式。这就是为什么 DeepSWE 对 OAI 模型如此慷慨，严格的任务执行是他们最擅长的。我认为 FrontierCode 更符合这种氛围。

children.author: justachillguy
children.textRawHtml: Naturally, given it’s their benchmark they have overfitted their model somewhat to it.
children.textZhHtml: 很自然，既然是他们自己的基准测试，他们的模型在某种程度上对它过拟合了。

children.author: famouswaffles
children.textRawHtml: Cursor sessions are pretty much what composer models are RL'd on. This bench and the training data are/should be basically the same distribution.
children.textZhHtml: Cursor的会话基本上就是Composer模型进行强化学习的数据。这个基准测试和训练数据基本上/应该是同一分布的。

children.author: muzani
children.textRawHtml: Anecdotally, I find Composer 2.5 to be useless. I do use light LLMs like Claude Haiku and some of Cursor's older free models, but Composer is negative productivity for me.
children.textZhHtml: 就个人体验而言，我觉得Composer 2.5毫无用处。我确实会用一些轻量级的大模型，比如Claude Haiku和Cursor的一些旧版免费模型，但Composer对我来说简直是负生产力。

children.author: maxdo
children.textRawHtml: The opposite , I use for everything like trigger and monitor a 10 steps release process using composer , a very capable model
children.textZhHtml: 恰恰相反，我什么都用它，比如用 Composer 触发和监控一个10步的发布流程，它是个非常强大的模型

children.author: vorticalbox
children.textRawHtml:
this is my finding too, i have moved to it fully for most of the plan/coding.

for most tasks is capable and very cheap, for a days worth of tasks is costing about $10
children.textZhHtml:
我也有同样的发现，我现在大部分规划和编码工作已经完全转到用它了。

对于大多数任务它都能胜任而且非常便宜，一天的工作量大概只要花10美元

children.author: urbsgpw
children.textRawHtml:
Same here, maybe I'm underusing it a bit, because for anything that is a bit more complex i tend to err on the safe side and go with anthropic, but i wonder if thats just a placebo effect because i pay more for it.

I do feel that they've really upped their game with composer this year though.
children.textZhHtml:
我也是，可能我有点没充分利用它，因为遇到稍微复杂点的任务，我倾向于稳妥起见选择 Anthropic，但我怀疑这会不会只是因为我花了更多钱而产生的安慰剂效应。

不过我确实觉得他们今年在 Composer 上真的有了质的飞跃。

children.author: datadrivenangel
children.textRawHtml: For lighter interactive agentic coding, where you type stuff into an IDE and a minute or three later get results back for review, composer 2.5 is honestly pretty great. The results get notably worse for larger tasks though.
children.textZhHtml: 对于较轻量的交互式代理编程，也就是你在IDE里输入内容，一两分钟后拿到结果进行审查，Composer 2.5说实话相当不错。不过对于更大的任务，结果就明显变差了。

children.author: anon7000
children.textRawHtml: Agreed. It’s worse than Opus of course. But Opus takes more than 10x longer to give you something to look at. I’m not kidding, I “benchmarked” a real ticket I was working on. Opus 4.7 took more than 30min. Opus 4.8 took over an hour. Composer 2.5 took 5min on the exact same prompt & local setup. My subjective review is that composer’s code was only like 10-20% worse. It still worked, it was just a bit less clean and a little more hacky. But it’s not like Opus is flawless either. At the end of the day, if it takes an hour to get to draft code I can look at and iterate on… that’s fucking impossible for me. Unless it did an excellent job. But as long as I still need to review and follow up with changes, Opus is just too slow. It’s really frustrating because it’s a lot slower than it was 6mo ago, and not noticeably better. Fable seems a step in the right direction but is $$$$
children.textZhHtml: 同意。当然，它比 Opus 差。但 Opus 生成能让你查看的代码要花10倍以上的时间。我没开玩笑，我拿手头一个真实的工单做了“基准测试”。Opus 4.7 花了30多分钟。Opus 4.8 花了一个多小时。Composer 2.5 在完全相同的提示词&本地环境下只用了5分钟。我的主观评价是，Composer 生成的代码只差了大概10-20%。代码依然能跑，只是没那么整洁，稍微有点拼凑感。但 Opus 也不是完美无缺的。说到底，如果要等一个小时才能拿到可以查看和迭代的草稿代码……那对我来说简直他妈的没法用。除非它一次就能做得非常完美。但只要我还需要审查并跟进修改，Opus 就太慢了。这真的很让人抓狂，因为它比6个月前慢了很多，而且并没有明显变好。Fable 似乎朝着正确的方向迈进了一步，但贵得要命

children.author: WinstonSmith84
children.textRawHtml: that benchmark seems to match my experience. GPT 5.5 is significantly better than Opus 4.8, last time I tried composer 2.5 it was truly dumb, and Fable to me looks to be on par with GPT 5.5 but .. different overall ... The best is to have a LLM-peer-review between GPT and Opus (now Fable) for best outcome.
children.textZhHtml: 该基准似乎符合我的经验。 GPT 5.5 明显优于 Opus 4.8，上次我尝试 Composer 2.5 时它确实很愚蠢，而《神鬼寓言》对我来说看起来与 GPT 5.5 相当，但……总体不同……最好的方法是在 GPT 和 Opus（现在的《神鬼寓言》）之间进行 LLM 同行评审，以获得最佳结果。

children.author: apothegm
children.textRawHtml: Composer writes the worst, stupidest, most naive and straight up brains-dead code you could imagine. Fast and cheap is about all it’s got going for it. I mostly use it for “sort these lines alphabetically” and stuff that’s a smidge too complex for regex find/replace.
children.textZhHtml: Composer 编写了你能想象到的最糟糕、最愚蠢、最天真、最直接的脑死亡代码。快速且便宜是它的全部优点。我主要用它来“按字母顺序对这些行进行排序”以及对于正则表达式查找/替换来说有点太复杂的东西。
